jokes.text: Dennis insisting on 'old-school' road trip with maps from the '70s while rejecting GPS
jokes.setup: Mac suggests getting a GPS for navigation
jokes.punchline: Dennis dramatically rejects modern convenience for 'authentic' experience using outdated maps
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's pretentiousness and obsession with image over practicality

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis insisting on 'old-school' road trip with maps from the '70s while rejecting GPS

jokes.text: 'Oh, shit... That's Sweet Dee?' - Gang's visible horror at seeing Dee arrive
jokes.setup: Gang spots Dee arriving in her new car
jokes.punchline: Their immediate panic and dread at her presence
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny dynamic - the gang's genuine disgust at each other's presence

dialogue.line: Oh, shit... That's Sweet Dee?

jokes.text: God damn it. What is that car. What is she doing in here? / Hey, guys!
jokes.setup: The guys are trying to sneak out before Dee arrives
jokes.punchline: Dee shows up cheerfully just as they're complaining about her arrival
jokes.explanation: Well-timed comedic juxtaposition between their dread and her oblivious enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: God damn it. What is that car. What is she doing in here?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hey, guys!

jokes.text: 'No i'm not scared. It's just, i haven't left Philly yet. Why i leave Philly?'
jokes.setup: Others claim Charlie is scared to leave Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: Charlie's circular logic questioning why anyone would leave Philly
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - his innocent question reveals deep provincial mindset

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No i'm not scared. It's just, i haven't left Philly yet. Why i leave Philly?

jokes.text: Frank wants to see it before he dies, so...
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're going to Grand Canyon
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Frank's mortality as trip motivation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, good economy of words

dialogue.line: Frank wants to see it before he dies, so...

jokes.text: we were hoping to get out here before your bust arrive
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why they're leaving early
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits they were trying to ditch Dee
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Mac accidentally reveals their plan

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: we were hoping to get out here before your bust arrive

jokes.text: 'I've never eaten a pear... Pears weird me out, dude. Where do you start with the pear? The top? The bottom?'
jokes.setup: Charlie reveals he's never eaten a pear
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about basic fruit consumption mechanics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - treating simple fruit eating as an existential puzzle

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I've never eaten a pear... Pears weird me out, dude. Where do you start with the pear? The top? The bottom?

jokes.text: 'Hey, i've never had blueberries either.'
jokes.setup: Group is already shocked by Charlie's pear revelation
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually drops another basic food he's never tried
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation technique - doubling down on the absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, i've never had blueberries either.

jokes.text: Hey, i've never had blueberries either.
jokes.setup: The pear revelation
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually drops that he's also never had blueberries
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'Charlie hasn't experienced basic things' running gag

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, i've never had blueberries either.

jokes.text: Dennis road raging at cyclist: 'Ride on the side walk if you're not ride a bike, asshole!'
jokes.setup: Cyclist is taking up road space
jokes.punchline: Dennis's illogical demand that bike rider not ride bike
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's entitled rage and poor logic under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Ride on the side walk if you're not ride a bike, asshole!

jokes.text: 'You know what? I'm gonna fire beer across his bow, give him a scare.'
jokes.setup: Dennis is angry at cyclist who flipped them off
jokes.punchline: Naval warfare terminology for throwing beer can at bike rider
jokes.explanation: Brilliant word choice - treating road rage like naval combat

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna fire beer across his bow, give him a scare.

jokes.text: He's getting something out of his bag. / What's he getting? / It's a pipe.
jokes.setup: Cyclist getting up after being hit with beer
jokes.punchline: He's retrieving a weapon to retaliate
jokes.explanation: Logical escalation of the conflict, creates tension and consequences for their actions

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's getting something out of his bag.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's he getting?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's a pipe.

jokes.text: 'Plus, there's a good deal on mountain bike in your axle here, bro.'
jokes.setup: Mechanic examining damaged car
jokes.punchline: Treating embedded bicycle as a sales opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating disaster as business opportunity

dialogue.speaker: Mechanic
dialogue.line: Plus, there's a good deal on mountain bike in your axle here, bro.

jokes.text: Hey! Hey you bastards! You jerks! / God damn it! Get out! / It's my car, i'm driving. / You jerks.
jokes.setup: Gang arguing about who drives Dee's car
jokes.punchline: Frank's over-the-top outrage at being left behind
jokes.explanation: Standard character conflict, shows their selfishness but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey you bastards! You jerks!

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: God damn it! Get out! It's my car, i'm driving.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You jerks.

jokes.text: Maybe we crack that door a little bit? You're gonna flying out at the first corner? I don't think so, buddy.
jokes.setup: Frank wanting fresh air in the trailer
jokes.punchline: Dee's concern about him flying out reveals how unsafe the setup is
jokes.explanation: Good moment highlighting the absurd danger of their trailer situation

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Maybe we crack that door a little bit?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're gonna flying out at the first corner? I don't think so, buddy.

jokes.text: 'These people are like descendants of gypsies, they come in, they sell their shit, they bang each other'
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining Italian market vendors
jokes.punchline: Casual racist stereotyping delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's casual bigotry and ignorance as character flaw

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: These people are like descendants of gypsies, they come in, they sell their shit, they bang each other

jokes.text: The weight of the door will keep the beer bottle in place.
jokes.setup: Using a beer bottle to prop open trailer door
jokes.punchline: Confident explanation of obviously flawed physics
jokes.explanation: Good example of character overconfidence in terrible engineering

dialogue.line: The weight of the door will keep the beer bottle in place.

jokes.text: Self-help tape: 'Inner peace is an attainable goal. The first step is releasing the anger and aggression that one hold inside...'
jokes.setup: Gang desperately needs music for road trip
jokes.punchline: Only available tape is new-age self-help, opposite of their nature
jokes.explanation: Nice irony having anger-management advice for these violent characters

dialogue.speaker: Self-help narrator
dialogue.line: Inner peace is an attainable goal. The first step is releasing the anger and aggression that one hold inside...

jokes.text: 'I don't have a bucket list, dude 'cause i'm not dying. Everybody's dying, bitch.'
jokes.setup: Frank mentions Charlie's bucket list
jokes.punchline: Charlie's denial of mortality immediately countered by Frank's harsh reality
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Charlie's innocent optimism and Frank's bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't have a bucket list, dude 'cause i'm not dying.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Everybody's dying, bitch.

jokes.text: Frank haggling: 'Those are 50 cents. I won't pay full price. So i'll give you dime.'
jokes.setup: Frank wants to buy a pear for 50 cents
jokes.punchline: His opening negotiation offer is 20% of asking price for fruit
jokes.explanation: Frank's extreme cheapness applied to smallest possible purchase

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Those are 50 cents. I won't pay full price. So i'll give you dime.

jokes.text: Tastes like sand. / That pear is not right, dude. He burned us.
jokes.setup: Charlie finally tries his first pear
jokes.punchline: It tastes terrible and they blame the vendor for scamming them
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the pear setup, their paranoid response to bad fruit is in character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Tastes like sand.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: That pear is not right, dude. He burned us.

jokes.text: 'I'm calling a lot of people bozo now. It's like my new thing.'
jokes.setup: Frank calls vendor 'bozo'
jokes.punchline: His proud announcement of adopting outdated insult as catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Frank's oblivious pride in using dated slang is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm calling a lot of people bozo now. It's like my new thing.

jokes.text: I can't, I just ate it. / The whole thing? / Yeah, it was pretty gross. The stem and then the core and the...
jokes.setup: Being asked to return the bad pear
jokes.punchline: Charlie ate the entire pear including inedible parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - of course he ate the stem and core, escalates his cluelessness perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I can't, I just ate it.
The whole thing?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was pretty gross. The stem and then the core and the...

jokes.text: 'I eat stickers all the time, dude!'
jokes.setup: Mac horrified that Charlie ate fruit stickers
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual revelation that this is normal behavior for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie line - escalates absurdity while revealing his bizarre everyday habits. Instantly quotable and iconic.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I eat stickers all the time, dude!

jokes.text: Frank, come on, the gipsy wants to barter, let's barter with him. / We don't have time to barter.
jokes.setup: Frank ready to pay full price
jokes.punchline: Other character insisting on offensive 'gypsy bartering' stereotype
jokes.explanation: Continues offensive stereotype from earlier, shows character consistency but relies on cheap shock

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank, come on, the gipsy wants to barter, let's barter with him.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We don't have time to barter.

jokes.text: Dennis's elaborate duct tape safety setup: 'I mesured the crack and the crack is smaller than the height of the grill.'
jokes.setup: Mac questioning Dennis's trailer safety measures
jokes.punchline: Dennis's overconfident engineering explanation for clearly dangerous setup
jokes.explanation: Dennis's false confidence in clearly inadequate safety measures

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I mesured the crack and the crack is smaller than the height of the grill.

jokes.text: State naming game: 'Maine. New Hampshire. Vermont. Philly. North Virginia. South Virginia. East Virginia.'
jokes.setup: Playing drinking game naming 50 states
jokes.punchline: Treating Philly as state, inventing fake Virginias, complete ignorance of geography
jokes.explanation: Reveals characters' stunning ignorance of basic American geography

dialogue.line: State naming game: 'Maine. New Hampshire. Vermont. Philly. North Virginia. South Virginia. East Virginia.'

jokes.text: Burning wicker chairs plan: 'What do you say we smash these wicker chairs to bits, we put them in the middle of the U-Haul, which is metal, and we burn the wicker chairs, cook the hotdogs on them.'
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to cook hotdogs on the road
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan to destroy furniture for cooking inside metal trailer
jokes.explanation: Typical gang logic - elaborate dangerous solution to simple problem

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What do you say we smash these wicker chairs to bits, we put them in the middle of the U-Haul, which is metal, and we burn the wicker chairs, cook the hotdogs on them.

jokes.text: I don't want anyone shrinking my head and shit. / Yeah. First of all, it was not gypsies ever that did that, That's a sort of witch-doctor thing.
jokes.setup: Concern about gypsies shrinking heads
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction about which stereotype is responsible for head shrinking
jokes.explanation: Pedantic correction of racist stereotypes is absurd but relies on offensive content

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't want anyone shrinking my head and shit.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. First of all, it was not gypsies ever that did that, That's a sort of witch-doctor thing.

jokes.text: 'Well, I mean, you know, gypsies. I don't want anyone shrinking my head and shit.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about visiting more 'gypsy hangout spots'
jokes.punchline: Mac's confused fear mixing up gypsies with witch doctors and head shrinking
jokes.explanation: Mac's ignorant mixing of ethnic stereotypes with movie tropes

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, you know, gypsies. I don't want anyone shrinking my head and shit.

jokes.text: Dee picking up hitchhiker: 'Sweet baby, they are gonna eat you alive in Hollywood. You're definitely gonna end up doing gay porn with this tiny little body of yours.'
jokes.setup: Young hitchhiker mentions going to Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate jump to graphic sexual exploitation prediction
jokes.explanation: Dee's inappropriate brutal honesty delivered with cruel casualness

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Sweet baby, they are gonna eat you alive in Hollywood. You're definitely gonna end up doing gay porn with this tiny little body of yours.

jokes.text: Trailer fire from 'poison glaze' on wicker chairs
jokes.setup: Plan to burn wicker chairs for cooking goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Chairs had mysterious toxic coating causing explosion and unconsciousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of earlier bad idea into complete disaster

dialogue.line: Trailer fire from 'poison glaze' on wicker chairs

jokes.text: Mac's 'ocular pat-down': 'I did an ocular assessment of the situation, garnered that he was not a security risk, and I cleared him for passage.'
jokes.setup: Frank questions picking up hitchhiker
jokes.punchline: Mac's elaborate pseudo-military jargon for looking at someone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Mac delusion - treating basic human observation as tactical security assessment

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I did an ocular assessment of the situation, garnered that he was not a security risk, and I cleared him for passage.

jokes.text: 'The sheriff of Paddy's.'
jokes.setup: Dennis asks how Mac views himself in the group
jokes.punchline: Mac's grandiose self-appointed law enforcement title for bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Mac's delusional self-importance

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The sheriff of Paddy's.

jokes.text: Charlie's explanation: 'Last thing i remember,we were at the gas station, and you guys hopped in back, and i hopped in the front with the hitchhiker. Then i got freaked out about leaving Philly, so i asked him if he drop me off back here'
jokes.setup: Gang wakes up back at bar, car missing
jokes.punchline: Charlie's innocent recounting of enabling their car theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Charlie's fear of leaving Philly causing group disaster

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Last thing i remember,we were at the gas station, and you guys hopped in back, and i hopped in the front with the hitchhiker. Then i got freaked out about leaving Philly, so i asked him if he drop me off back here

jokes.text: He didn't take us to the Grand Canyon, he detached the trailer, and stole my car.
jokes.setup: Charlie thought the hitchhiker was helping them
jokes.punchline: Hitchhiker robbed them and left them in a parking lot
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff - Charlie's trust was completely misplaced, consequences feel earned

dialogue.line: He didn't take us to the Grand Canyon, he detached the trailer, and stole my car.

jokes.text: 'The weird thing is, Mac had already cleared him as a security risk. Did an ocular pat-down on him.'
jokes.setup: Car stolen by hitchhiker Mac supposedly vetted
jokes.punchline: Frank sarcastically referencing Mac's failed 'security assessment'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing complete failure of Mac's security theater

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The weird thing is, Mac had already cleared him as a security risk. Did an ocular pat-down on him.

jokes.text: The weird thing is, Mac had already cleared him as a security risk. Did an ocular pat-down on him. / Amazing, right? / Are you cleared him, Mac? / I cleared him. / I thought he was clear. / He wasn't clear.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they got robbed
jokes.punchline: Mac's 'security clearance' was completely wrong and useless
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that proves Mac's security assessment was worthless, undercuts his authority

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The weird thing is, Mac had already cleared him as a security risk. Did an ocular pat-down on him.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Amazing, right?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you cleared him, Mac?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I cleared him.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I thought he was clear.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He wasn't clear.

jokes.text: Continuing state game: 'Texas! Ohio. Rhode Island. Detroit. That's not a state. Milwaukee.'
jokes.setup: Back to drinking and state naming game
jokes.punchline: Still completely failing at basic geography, naming cities as states
jokes.explanation: Solid callback showing they learned nothing from their adventure

dialogue.line: Texas! Ohio. Rhode Island. Detroit. That's not a state. Milwaukee.
